Off Calendar Mar-09-2020 Continued To Jun-03-20 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-15-2020 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Roshni C. Kapoor, Esq. of OGLETREE DEAKINS NASH SMOAK & STEWART PC, T:415.536.3403, for defendant/moving party;
Ryan J. Larsen, Esq. of KATTEN MUCHIN ROSENMAN LLP, T:310.788.4544, for plaintiff/opposing party.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on DEFENDANT DIGITAL REALTY TRUST, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT, as follows:

Defendant Digital Realty Trust, Inc.'s demurrer to the complaint is overruled. The complaint adequately alleges the existence of an actual controversy between the parties regarding the enforceability of the non-compete provision in the parties' Employee Confidentiality and Covenant Agreement. (See Compl. 17 & Ex. B, 19-25.) Indeed, it is undisputed that Defendant is attempting to enforce that provision in the Texas action, and Plaintiff is attempting to invalidate it in this action. The Texas action "does not divest California of jurisdiction, and [Plaintiffs] remain[] free to litigate the California action unless and until [Defendant] demonstrates to the [San Francisco] County Superior Court that any [Texas] judgment is binding on the parties." (Advanced Bionics Corp. v. Medtronic, Inc.(2002) 29 Cal.4th 697, 708.)

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
